g.text: Milestone Index — Event Loop with epoll
M1 | build-event-loop-m1 | epoll Basics LT and ET echo server
M2 | build-event-loop-m2 | Write Buffering and Timer Heap
M3 | build-event-loop-m3 | Reactor API and Callback Dispatch
M4 | build-event-loop-m4 | HTTP/1.1 Server — C10K Benchmark
linked list | fds ready since last wait
O(1) event delivery regardless of fd count
signal | recv empty or send full
EAGAIN = not an error; try again on next event
fd number is array index (O(1) lookup)
read once per event; epoll re-notifies if data remains
MUST drain to EAGAIN; single read = silent data loss
LT | yes, if data remains in buffer
ET | no, only on state transition
ET | NGINX, Node.js libuv, this project M3+
socket() flag | listen socket must be non-blocking
socket() flag | no fd leak on fork/exec
epoll_create1 flag | no fd leak on exec
128 | kernel queued connections; not conn limit
append compact triggers memmove when offset > capacity/2
write() EAGAIN or partial: arm EPOLLOUT
write_buf drains to 0: MUST disarm EPOLLOUT
ORDER IS MANDATORY — prevents fd-reuse race
heap_swap MUST update heap_idx AND conn->timer_id
I/O before timers: data receipt resets timer first
int | opaque ID returned to caller
I/O Dispatch | dispatching=1; call cb per event
Apply Pending | epoll_ctl ADD/MOD/DEL; clear slots
dispatching=0 in phases 2-4; ops execute immediately
EPOLLET always set; user code never calls epoll_ctl
ring buf; snapshot tail before drain; re-deferred runs next tick
void (*)(int fd, uint32_t events, void* udata)
4 | response sent; wait for next request
stateless: examines entire read_buf on every call
parser never calls read(); only examines read_buf
call after any number of bytes appended
pipelined bytes moved to read_buf[0] on COMPLETE
realpath + prefix check + boundary char check
close: state=CLOSING FIRST; deferred free via reactor_defer
10000ms | fires if CRLF CRLF never arrives
both timers reset after response sent
32 bytes = 0.5 cache lines — fits in L1
borrow checker = dispatching flag (runtime vs compile)
completion model vs readiness; same reactor_t API
zero-copy: page_cache → send_buf, no userspace copy
EAGAIN chain: slow_peer → recv_buf → window → EAGAIN
